steps.title: Find the shared library model
steps.content: The passage contrasts private Roman libraries with early public ones, then pivots to Marcos Pereira's point that they were not as distinct as they seemed. Both were viewed as extensions of personal patronage, though on different scales.

steps.title: Reject absent claims
steps.content: A would be an overview of republican library access, B would judge imperial influence as excessive, and D would discuss disappearance of private libraries. Those ideas are absent. The passage's main purpose is to assert an essential similarity between private and early public libraries. The answer is C.
